target.name: Mission Zero Technologies
target.founder: Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, Gaël Gobaille-Shaw

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mission Zero Technologies is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, and Gaël Gobaille-Shaw, having raised £21.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with primary focus on three same-sector, same-stage peers: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The three comparables yield a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This methodology directly reflects market pricing for climate and energy companies at Series A, controlling for both sector and development stage. The limited number of comps reflects the scarcity of late-stage funding data in this sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on $150M. This range reflects the interquartile positioning relative to observed peers and incorporates downside and upside sensitivity around the median. Key uncertainties include the company's specific technology maturity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and market timing—the climate and energy sector has experienced significant volatility in private funding availability. The single applicable method limits confidence; additional data on burn rate, technical progress, or customer traction would narrow the range materially.
